title: The Ship Approaches the Desert
description: On pages 0–9, this part focuses on the ship approaches the desert, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: The Ship Approaches the Desert. After leaving Drum, the crew heads deeper into Alabasta’s conflict while the desert kingdom’s danger comes into view. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How the hell am I supposed to sleep like this?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Luffy! It's because you keep eating our bait!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: How do you expect us to catch anything with no bait!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It has been 5 days since the crew left Drum Island.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That was only the itty bitty bits stuck to the bottom of the lid!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The ship sails onward to Alabasta, albeit with some slight troubles.

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Luffy, Usop! Have you caught anything Y-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Gonna catch me a sea king,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: What're you two doing to Carue!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Nami, come quick! There's some- thing ahead!

summary: This part highlights the ship approaches the desert and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
